title: Bridge Essay: Spoken language processing activates the primary visual cortex.
concept: Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Spoken language processing activates the primary visual cortex.** - PLoS One (2023). DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0289671. PMID: 37566582. Primary visual cortex (V1) is generally thought of as a low-level sensory area that primarily processes basic visual features. Although there is evidence for multisensory effects on its activity, these are typically found for the processing of simple sounds and their properties, for example spatially or temporally-congruent simple sounds.
